clusters.headline: South Africa: Cape Flats Residents Fight Extortionists - South African News Briefs - July 17, 2026
Algeria: Fire at orphanage kills 11, including children
clusters.summary: A fire at an orphanage in the province of Algiers has killed 11 people and injured 19 more, authorities said. President Abdelmadjid Tebboune expressed his condolences amid his trip to Berlin.

clusters.headline: China rebukes UK over nationalisation of British Steel
clusters.summary: The UK has appropriated its last working steelworks following fears its former Chinese owners would shut it down.

clusters.headline: School bus crash kills at least 20 pupils in Uganda
clusters.summary: Dozens others, including school staff, were injured in the crash, suspected to have been caused by a mechanical fault.

clusters.headline: Forest City’s Network School says US$122m investment plan on hold amid Malaysia’s probe into alleged Israeli link
clusters.summary: Locals in Johor’s Forest City tell CNA the startup community has boosted businesses but add that anyone found breaching Malaysia's immigration laws should face action.

clusters.headline: Actress Fann Wong no longer owns a weighing scale: 'Having strength and energy is much more important'
clusters.summary: The 55-year-old, who used to feel "upset" when her weight went above 49kg, shared in a recent interview how her outlook on health and weight has changed over the years.

clusters.headline: South Africa: Ramaphosa Pleads 'Humiliation' in Bid to Stop Impeachment Inquiry in Its Tracks
clusters.summary: [Daily Maverick] Cyril Ramaphosa claims ongoing impeachment proceedings will lead to significant humiliation, as the High Court weighs his urgent application to halt the inquiry into the Phala Phala allegations.

clusters.headline: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
clusters.summary: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.

clusters.headline: Chief Justice Sundaresh Menon to retire after 14 years, Justice Sushil Nair named successor
clusters.summary: Court of Appeal judge Sushil Sukumaran Nair will become Singapore's fifth Chief Justice on Feb 26, 2027.

clusters.headline: Uganda: WHO Warns DR Congo Ebola Outbreak Is Outpacing Response As Uganda Reaches Milestone
clusters.summary: [UN News] The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has become the third largest on record and is spreading faster than any previous one during the first month of exposure, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Thursday.

clusters.headline: Uganda: Kapchorwa Bus Tragedy - 23 Ndejje Pupils Feared Dead After School Tour Turns Fatal
clusters.summary: [New Light] Kapchorwa: At least 23 pupils of King David Junior School Ndejje are feared dead following a road accident in Kapchorwa District on Thursday evening, as the pupils travelled for a field trip whose exact destination remained unclear at the time of the crash.

clusters.headline: Explosions heard in Qatar, sirens sound off in Bahrain as Iran claims attacks in Kuwait
clusters.summary:
Explosions heard in Qatar, sirens sound off in Bahrain as Iran claims attacks in Kuwait

    
    

        
    
                Qatar's defence ministry indicated that it is intercepting a number of aerial attacks, after several reports of blasts heard in the capital of Doh
